title: Rejection

shortDialogue.speaker: 준서
shortDialogue.english: Ju-hee, do you have a boyfriend?

longDialogue.speaker: 준서
longDialogue.english: Ju-hee, do you have a boyfriend?

longDialogue.speaker: 주희
longDialogue.english: What? What are you talking about?

longDialogue.speaker: 준서
longDialogue.korean: 저 주희 씨 좋아해요. 주희 씨랑 만나고 싶어요.
longDialogue.english: Ju-hee, I like you. I want to go out with you.

longDialogue.speaker: 주희
longDialogue.korean: 네? 우리 아직 한 번밖에 안 만났어요.
longDialogue.english: What? We've only met once so far.

longDialogue.speaker: 준서
longDialogue.korean: 알아요. 그래도 전 주희 씨가 너무 좋아요. 주희 씨는 제가 싫어요?
longDialogue.english: I know. But still, I like you very much, Ju-hee. Do you not like me?

longDialogue.speaker: 주희
longDialogue.korean: 저는 아직 준서 씨 잘 몰라요.
longDialogue.english: Jun-seo, I don't know you that well yet.

longDialogue.speaker: 준서
longDialogue.english: You will slowly get to know me.

longDialogue.speaker: 주희
longDialogue.english: No, I don't want to date you yet.

culturalTip: The native Korean word, 사귀다 [sa-gwi-da], commonly translates as "to go out" or "to date" in English. However, there are also the loanwords 데이트 [de-i-teu], which means "a date", and 데이트하다, meaning "to go on a date". Although these three words seem to be interchangeable, they are definitely used in specific situations to imply different things. 데이트 or 데이트하다 are used when two people are meeting one-on-one or are not officially a couple, while 사귀다 is used to indicate that two people are a couple and have gone out on dates before.
koreanReview:
- Short Dialogue
준서: 주희 씨, 남자 친구 있어요?
주희: 아니요. 없어요.

- Long Dialogue
준서: 주희 씨, 남자 친구 있어요?
주희: 아니요. 없어요.
준서: 그래요? 그럼 저는 어때요?
주희: 네? 무슨 말이에요?
준서: 저 주희 씨 좋아해요. 주희 씨랑 만나고 싶어요.
주희: 네? 우리 아직 한 번밖에 안 만났어요.
준서: 알아요. 그래도 전 주희 씨가 너무 좋아요. 주희 씨는 제가 싫어요?
주희: 아니요. 싫지 않아요.
준서: 그럼 우리 사귀어요.
주희: 저는 아직 준서 씨 잘 몰라요.
준서: 천천히 알게 될 거예요.
주희: 아니요. 저는 아직 사귀기 싫어요.

coreVocab.meaning: honorific suffix which implies respect toward the person whose name it is attached to
to not exist, to not have, to not be there

extendedVocab.meaning: to not exist, to not have, to not be there

grammar.title: Making a Suggestion to Include Yourself
grammar.explanation: Used to make a suggestion that includes the speaker.

grammar.title: To Not Like/Want To Do Something
grammar.pattern: VERB + -기 싫어요. = I don't like VERB-ing, I don't want to VERB.
grammar.meaning: I don't like VERB-ing, I don't want to VERB.
grammar.explanation: Used to express that you do not like or want to do something.

examples.english: I don't want to go out (with you).

pronunciation.rule: When a syllable ends with ㅀ and is followed by a syllable that begins with a vowel, only the ㄹ is pronounced while ㅎ is not pronounced.

When a syllable ends with ㅀ and is followed by a syllable that begins with ㅈ, the ㄹ is pronounced, but the ㅎ changes the pronunciation of ㅈ to ㅊ. ㅎ is not pronounced because it is used to create the ㅊ sound.
